notes: Evidence-only adapter: no Logic project file is parsed; the DAW is never observed live, written to, or asked to render.
Role inference is HEURISTIC with calibrated confidences: 99.3% over 6467 weighted filename instances (in-sample vocabulary coverage, not held-out generalization — see docs/evaluation.md).
Empty write/live_observation/render sections mean support NONE for every domain, not an unexamined gap.

hidden_state_markers.consequence: Temporal mix decisions such as vocal rides, send throws, or filter sweeps may be audible as a printed gain or timbre envelope, but the point-by-point curves and their targets are no longer editable DAW state.
hidden_state_markers.description: Automation state — Logic's track automation and region automation curves, their parameter targets, and per-track mode (Read/Touch/Latch/Write) — is not available from exported stems unless separately documented.

hidden_state_markers.consequence: The graph may represent exported stems as flat tracks even if the original Logic session used subgroup buses, track stacks, or VCA faders. The structures themselves are unrecoverable: a mixer group links controls without touching signal flow, while a VCA fader or a folder stack's stack master (which functions as a VCA master fader) imprints gain into each stem indistinguishably from individual fader moves.
hidden_state_markers.description: Routing state — sends (Pre Fader / Post Fader / Post Pan) to bus-fed aux channel strips, summing and folder track stacks, VCA group assignments, mixer groups, and sidechain sources — is not reliably recoverable from stem audio alone.

recommendations.caveat: A reference is a point of comparison, not an objective target.
recommendations.explanation: A stereo mixdown is available, but no reference track was supplied. A reference could help compare spectral balance, dynamics, and production intent without treating one mix as objectively correct.
recommendations.suggested_action: Upload a reference track and add a short note about what qualities it represents.
recommendations.title: Reference-aware evaluation could be added.

recommendations.caveat: Name matching is heuristic and may miss valid pairings.
recommendations.explanation: MIDI or score evidence contains parts that do not clearly match an exported stem (e.g. Drummer, Drummer, Drummer). Linking them would improve the relationship between musical structure and acoustic outcome.
recommendations.suggested_action: Rename stems consistently or add a manifest mapping score/MIDI parts to exported audio files.
recommendations.title: Some musical parts are not linked to audio evidence.

recommendations.caveat: A high residual can also come from missing stems or misaligned exports; it identifies a gap, not its cause.
recommendations.explanation: Summing the exported stems and fitting a single gain leaves a residual of -0.02 dB relative to the mixdown; the residual is largest in 500-1000Hz, 250-500Hz, 1000-2000Hz. This is signal evidence that bus or master processing, automation, or additional content separates the stem exports from the final mix.
recommendations.suggested_action: Document any bus/master processing, or re-export full-length, bar-1-aligned stems if the mismatch is unexpected.
recommendations.title: Mixdown contains processing not present in the stem sum.
